summary: Split the former sysdolphin/baselib hsd_3B34 range into two translation units, hsd_3B34.c and new hsd_3B5C.c, around a plausible TU boundary at hsd_803B5C4C. The PR added large nonmatching C implementations and JPEG-related static data tables for encode/decode routines, kept hsd_803B5C2C matching at 100%, updated GALE01 split section ranges, added hsd_3B5C.c to configure.py as NonMatching, and adjusted hsd_3B34.h. The rationale was grounded in clean extab/extabindex cut points and data-section separation between the first JPEG table cluster and a later large table cluster.

key_files.role: Existing TU rewritten with nonmatching C and JPEG tables for the first part of the former range; text range now ends at 0x803B5C4C.
New split TU containing the later JPEG decode-side helper cluster beginning at 0x803B5C4C.
Header updated for the split routines and declarations.
Adjusted extab, extabindex, text, data, and sdata2 ranges for hsd_3B34.c and added the new hsd_3B5C.c split.
Added Object(NonMatching, "sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3B5C.c") to the build configuration.

what_changed: Changed hsd_3B34.c from one broad range ending at text 0x803B7230 into a shorter TU ending at text 0x803B5C4C.
Added sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3B5C.c as a new NonMatching object for the text range 0x803B5C4C through 0x803B7230.
Split extab/extabindex ranges at 0x80005690 and 0x800058E8 respectively, matching the proposed TU boundary.
Split .data at 0x80431090 and .sdata2 at 0x804DEB90, leaving hsd_3B34 with the first static JPEG table cluster and hsd_3B5C with a later table/constant cluster.
Added large static JPEG/Huffman/quantization-style byte and halfword tables such as lbl_803B9670, lbl_80430C40, and lbl_80430C80 in hsd_3B34.c.
Added nonmatching C for JPEG encode/decode routines, including hsd_803B3408 and additional static helper functions in hsd_3B34.c, with many m2c-style temporaries still present.
Preserved hsd_803B5C2C as a small end-of-first-cluster routine and reported it as matching at 100%.
Reported fuzzy match status of 84.855255% for hsd_3B34 and 84.95146% for hsd_3B5C.
Verified with pre-commit on the touched source/header files and a configure/build run using wibo and ninja.
smart_moves: Used section-boundary evidence rather than only function-address convenience to justify the TU split: extab, extabindex, .data, and .sdata2 all had clean adjacent cut points.
Kept the already-good hsd_803B5C2C match at the end of the first cluster instead of forcing it into the new decode-side TU.
Separated static JPEG table ownership by data-section clusters, reducing the chance that data ordering would be distorted by a purely text-driven split.
Added the new TU to configure.py as NonMatching immediately, making the split build-system-visible and preserving link/check workflow.
Documented fuzzy match percentages and verification commands in the PR body, giving reviewers a clear baseline for future cleanup.
decomp_lessons: For sysdolphin/baselib blob ranges, plausible TU splits should be checked against extab/extabindex boundaries and data-section boundaries, not just against function starts.
A matching short function at the edge of a cluster can be useful evidence for a TU boundary when neighboring functions/data form a distinct cluster.
Large static tables in JPEG-like code may be better assigned by contiguous data-section ownership than by nearby code references alone.
When introducing broad nonmatching C, keeping symbol names/address-derived labels intact preserves searchability and makes later incremental matching possible.
A TU split should be accompanied by synchronized edits to splits.txt and configure.py; otherwise the source addition alone is not enough for the build/link model.
naming_conventions: New split file follows the address-based baselib naming pattern: hsd_3B34.c for the range beginning at hsd_803B3408 and hsd_3B5C.c for the range beginning at hsd_803B5C4C.
Unknown functions and labels retain address-derived names such as hsd_803B3408, hsd_803B5C2C, lbl_803B9670, lbl_80430C40, and lbl_804D6398.
Static helper functions can remain auto-generated/address-derived when semantics are not yet confirmed, as seen with names like fn_803B376C.

m2c-style temporary names such as temp_r22, var_r10, temp_f3, and unused_arg4 were left in place in the nonmatching code, indicating this PR prioritized structural split and fuzzy progress over semantic renaming.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use clean adjacent extab/extabindex ranges as a strong indicator for translation-unit boundaries.
Check .data and .sdata2 start/end addresses for independent constant/table clusters before finalizing a split.
Keep a known 100% matching function at the side of the boundary where section ownership and clustering indicate it belongs.
Introduce nonmatching C with exact static data arrays and address labels first, then refine functions in later PRs.
Use build/link verification after modifying splits.txt to corroborate that rebuilt sections still align.
For pointer-heavy scratch-buffer code, the PR used an explicit buffer macro, HSD_804D2648_BUF, and m2c field accesses to express offsets before deeper struct recovery.

review_feedback.text: No inline review comments or formal review feedback were present in the provided slice.
The PR was merged quickly after creation, suggesting the split rationale and verification were accepted, but the slice does not include reviewer reasoning.

follow_up_queries: Search for later PRs that improve hsd_3B34.c or hsd_3B5C.c fuzzy matches beyond the initial 84.8% range.
Look for commits that rename hsd_803B3408, hsd_803B5C2C, fn_803B376C, or the JPEG table labels to semantic names.
Check whether hsd_804D2648 and HSD_804D2648_BUF were later replaced by a recovered struct for the JPEG scratch/work buffer.
Investigate later review or cleanup around the hsd_803B5C4C TU boundary to confirm whether the split remained stable.
Search for additional sysdolphin/baselib JPEG decode-side helpers in hsd_3B5C.c and whether their data tables were typed more precisely.
